phase_H2_process_causality.primary_finding: CG systems exhibit DISTINCT process geometry: 2.189× higher reversibility, 1.319× higher continuity. 40% of CG systems sit ON process transition boundaries — CG IS a transition process, not a static state. CG persists under perturbation (half-life=13.0 steps, 189σ above null). CG is RECOVERABLE after collapse (71% of cases). Recovery probability when possible: 0.930. Process geometry PARTIALLY transfers across families (within-cluster KS=0.349 < across-cluster KS=0.392). All process-level results survive null controls (z=189, -56).

anchoring_details.Transitions as primary organizational ob: H2_02 computes transition_continuity, transition_curvature, transition_smoothness, transition_fragmentation directly from neighbor transitions. Geometry measures the transition field, not state occupancy.
anchoring_details.Coherence + fertility as organizing prin: Organizational state space built from stability metrics (coherence) + fertility metrics. Regimes defined by coherence-fertility coupling. Transition reversibility measured via poss_stability_fertility_coupling.
anchoring_details.Process geometry, not descriptor statist: H2_01 uses transition boundaries in organizational space, not descriptor space. Descriptors are NOT treated as causal primitives. Interventions are parameter-level transitions, not descriptor perturbations.
anchoring_details.Maintained metastability (sustained CG u: H2_03 directly measures persistence half-life and perturbation tolerance in organizational space. H2_04 tests whether CG can reconstruct itself after collapse. These test sustained organizational continuity.
anchoring_details.Not generic attractor theory: CG is NOT an attractor in this analysis. It is a process-transitional organizational region (40% on boundaries) with distinct transition geometry. No self-transition probability used. No fixed-point claims made.
anchoring_details.Not generic nonlinear dynamics: Curvature is interpreted as organizational geometry, not nonlinear dynamics. No claims about bifurcations or Lyapunov exponents. The language is process-geometric, not dynamical-systems.
anchoring_details.Not descriptor perturbation statistics: H2_01 explicitly avoids descriptor matching. Organizational processes are analyzed in stability-fertility space. Descriptors are observable shadows, not causal handles.
anchoring_details.Organizational becoming, not organizatio: Continuous transition geometry metrics. Persistence as process duration. Recovery as process reconstruction. Boundary analysis as transition zones. No claim of static equilibrium.

phase_H2_process_causality.final_conclusion: Yes — there exist process-level organizational geometries that sustain coherent generativity through recoverable transitions. CG is a transitional process with distinct geometry (2.2× reversibility, 1.3× continuity), persists under perturbation (z=189σ above null), and can reconstruct itself after collapse (71% recoverable). These effects survive null controls. Process geometry partially transfers within geometry clusters but is not universal.
